characters.description: The farmer at the center of Fa Ram and Meiling's husband. He is expanding the farm's harvests, workshops, and practical machinery, but remains uneasy about the conflict that followed the Dueling Peaks.
characters.name: Jin

characters.description: Jin's pregnant wife, a healer and leader within Fa Ram. She is preparing a difficult operation to repair Liu Bowu's damaged leg, combining medicinal chi, spiritual herbs, anesthesia, and careful planning.
characters.name: Meiling

characters.description: An awakened rooster, Jin's first disciple, and a defender of Fa Ram. Seeing Jin struggle with the prospect of violence, he resolves that the disciples must become strong enough to share that burden.
characters.name: Bi De

characters.description: An awakened cat and one of Fa Ram's fighters. She notices Jin's troubled approach to combat training, supports the household's efforts to help him, and remains especially close to Cai Xiulan.
characters.name: Tigu

characters.description: A cultivator regarded by Jin as sworn family. She is rebuilding her cultivation after a severe earlier injury and joins Tigu and Bi De in helping Jin find a workable way to train.
characters.name: Cai Xiulan

characters.description: A grower, builder, and increasingly capable organizer at Fa Ram. He works with Liu Bowu on the farm's machinery and helps turn Jin's practical ideas into functioning projects.
characters.name: Gou Ren

characters.description: A young mortal inventor living at Fa Ram with a badly damaged leg. He contributes to the drop hammer and other mechanical work while Meiling and Ri Zu prepare a possible treatment for his chronic injury.
characters.name: Liu Bowu

characters.description: An awakened rat, healer, and Meiling's medical apprentice. She assists in assessing Liu Bowu's injured leg and is part of the team developing a treatment that ordinary healing methods cannot provide.
characters.name: Ri Zu

characters.description: An artist and Fa Ram ally who records images of medicinal plants for Meiling. He carries the intelligent sword Summer's Sky, which is treated as a companion within the household.
characters.name: Yun Ren

characters.description: The sentient sword carried by Yun Ren. Its intelligence is openly recognized at Fa Ram, where it is included as a member of the household rather than regarded as an ordinary weapon.
characters.name: Summer's Sky

characters.description: An injured awakened snake and skilled glassmaker at Fa Ram. With Yin supplying precisely controlled flame, he is cautiously returning to a craft that once defined his life.
characters.name: Miantiao

characters.description: An awakened rabbit and Fa Ram artisan. She uses her controlled fire to support Miantiao's glassmaking, making her an important partner in one of the farm's new industries.
characters.name: Yin

characters.description: Liu Bowu's older sister, who holds the young-mistress position in the Misty Lake Sect. She comes to Hong Yaowu after privately seeking permission from Gou Ren's parents to court him.
characters.name: Liu Xianghua

characters.description: A reclusive monkey artisan whose attention is centered on Bi De's ancient crystal. He nevertheless joins Fa Ram's festival project, helping Tigu carve the household's animated displays.
characters.name: Hua Tan

characters.description: A member of Fa Ram who features in Hong Yaowu's Mid-Autumn Festival dragon dance.
characters.name: Wa Shi

characters.description: Meiling's father and Jin's father-in-law. He regards Jin as his son.
characters.name: Hong Xian

characters.description: Verdant Hill's civic leader. He is wary of the strain created by Jin's roads, visiting cultivators, and growing commercial influence when Jin approaches him to request advice.
characters.name: The Lord Magistrate

characters.description: The Lord Magistrate's wife. Care from Hong Yaowu and Meiling has eased the tremors and loss of sensation left by a cultivator.
characters.name: Lady Wu

characters.description: A friend of Jin and Meiling in Verdant Hill and the mother of an infant son.
characters.name: Meihua

characters.description: An awakened boar who handles stable work at Fa Ram. Visiting traders quickly recognize that the quiet household worker is also an exceptionally formidable guardian.
characters.name: Chun Ke

characters.description: A senior disciple of the Cloudy Sword Sect who succeeded in finding Jin. He reports that he honored Jin's refusal to return, considers him non-hostile, and has retained a route for future contact.
characters.name: Lu Ri

characters.description: The damaged earth spirit linked to Fa Ram's golden network. Frightened that others will exploit her essence, she has struggled alone until Jin, Meiling, and the household answer her need for shelter through the winter.
characters.name: Tianlan

characters.description: The ruler of Fa Ram's hives. She prepares her colony for winter by preserving veteran bees and treats Jin and Bi De as the powerful protectors of her domain.
characters.name: The bee queen

characters.description: Chun Ke's partner and a servant of Jin's household. While helping build the winter snow constructs, she consciously restrains the destructive impulses stirred by threats to Chun Ke.
characters.name: Pipa

characters.description: A greater fox spirit who appears through an avatar and identifies Yun Ren as his nephew. He is intrigued by Fa Ram and warns that Jin's ancient earth-bound cultivation may favor land and community without granting immortality.
characters.name: The fox-spirit relative

characters.description: The central figure in the crystal's ancient record. Exploited by an imperial overseer and his son, he abandons conventional cultivation for farming, where his gratitude toward the land draws the attention of an earth spirit.
characters.name: The past farmer

characters.description: The guardian of an ancient ruler's recorded history. It offers Bi De succession, lost knowledge, and the power attached to that inheritance.
characters.name: The ancient crystal spirit

characters.description: A retired cultivator and diviner living in Verdant Hill. She tells Bi De that repeated visions led her to seek a suitable person for the crystal, and that she rejected other candidates before giving him the map.
characters.name: The crone

characters.description: A student of Bi De who intervenes when a drunk threatens a local child in Verdant Hill. He then spars with Liu Bowu and begins a friendly exchange of martial and craft knowledge.
characters.name: Zheng Fei

characters.description: A former cultivator in a gang that shelters poor, elderly, and injured people. Supported by his sworn brother, he is using reparations from the Shrouded Mountain Sect to repair his damaged cultivation.
characters.name: Loud Boy

characters.description: Jin's adoptive grandfather, an Imperial-realm cultivator and former member of the Cloudy Sword Sect. He receives Jin's letter rejecting conventional cultivation and plans to visit after the coming demon campaign.
characters.name: Jin's grandfather

characters.description: The healthy unnamed son of Jin and Meiling, born after a rapid and uncomplicated labor.
characters.name: Jin and Meiling's son

characters.description: The household nickname given to Jin and Meiling's still formally unnamed infant after he imitates Big D's morning crow. The name marks his place within the extended Fa Ram family.
characters.name: Little D
